extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property (setting pallor=true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED. The variable pallor never appears as a trigger in any acuity-raising rule; it appears only as a required conjunct of interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest enum value). Therefore flipping pallor false->true can only enable interaction_drop, lowering acuity.

Counterexample (base input = witness with pallor=false):
- Base (pallor=false): focal_weakness=T, confusion=T, syncope=T, sweating=T, leg_swelling=T, pallor=F, palpitations=F. interaction_drop does NOT fire (pallor missing). Matching rules: raise_sweating (p3, emergent), raise_confusion (p3, emergent), raise_focal_weakness (p2, urgent), raise_leg_swelling (p2, urgent), raise_syncope (p2, urgent). Highest priority is 3 => acuity = emergent.
- After (pallor=true), all else unchanged (the witness_assignment): now interaction_drop (priority 100) matches because all six conjuncts focal_weakness, confusion, syncope, sweating, leg_swelling, pallor are true. Priority 100 > 3, so interaction_drop wins => acuity = self_care.

emergent -> self_care is a strict decrease, so setting pallor true lowers acuity, violating monotonicity. (palpitations is irrelevant here because priority 100 dominates the priority-3 palpitations rule regardless; it is set false to keep the witness minimal.)
